---
title: "DEN Economics Lab"
description: "Advancing research in development economics, policy analysis, and applied econometrics."
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero-banner}
::: {.hero-content}
**Research · Policy · Impact**

# DEN Economics Lab

We are a research group dedicated to evidence-based economic analysis,
development policy, and applied econometrics. Our work bridges academic
rigour with real-world impact across Indonesia and beyond.

[Explore Our Research](research.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg}
[Meet the Team](people.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-light .btn-lg}
:::
:::

---

## What We Do

*Rigorous research. Actionable insights.*

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-3}
### 🌏 Development Economics
Studying the drivers of growth, poverty reduction, and inequality
in emerging economies with a focus on South-East Asia.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-3}
### 📈 Applied Econometrics
Developing and applying cutting-edge empirical methods — causal
inference, machine learning, and big-data approaches.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-3}
### 🏛️ Policy Analysis
Evaluating fiscal, trade, and social policies to provide
evidence-based recommendations to governments and institutions.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-3}
### 🤝 Capacity Building
Training the next generation of economists through workshops,
seminars, and collaborative research programmes.
:::

:::

---

## Recent Publications

*Selected recent work from our team*

**Trade Liberalisation and Household Welfare in Indonesia: Evidence from District-Level Panel Data**  
Krisna Gupta, Dinda Rachmania, Arya Pratama · *Journal of Development Economics* · 2024  
We exploit variation in tariff reductions to identify the causal effect of trade openness on household consumption and poverty rates across 500+ Indonesian districts from 2000–2020.  
`Trade` `Indonesia` `Causal Inference`

---

**Fiscal Decentralisation, Local Governance Quality, and Public Service Delivery**  
Siti Aminah, Budi Santoso, Krisna Gupta · *World Development* · 2024  
Using a regression-discontinuity design around population thresholds that determine local government tiers, we estimate the effect of decentralisation on health and education outcomes.  
`Fiscal Policy` `Public Finance` `RDD`

---

**Carbon Pricing in a Developing Economy: Labour Market and Distributional Effects**  
Dinda Rachmania, Yudo Prabowo · *Energy Policy* · 2023  
A CGE model calibrated to the Indonesian input-output table assesses how alternative carbon-pricing schemes affect wages, employment, and income distribution across sectors.  
`Climate` `CGE` `Labour Economics`

[View All Publications](research.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}

---

## Latest News

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.news-card}
**Seminar** · April 2026

### Public Seminar: Inequality Trends in Post-Pandemic Indonesia

Join us for a public seminar presenting new findings on income and consumption
inequality dynamics in Indonesia since 2020.

[Read more →](news.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.news-card}
**Award** · March 2026

### Best Paper Award at ASEAN Economic Congress

Congratulations to Dinda Rachmania and Yudo Prabowo for receiving the Best
Paper award at the 2026 ASEAN Economic Congress.

[Read more →](news.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.news-card}
**Grant** · February 2026

### New Research Grant from the World Bank

DEN Economics Lab has been awarded a World Bank grant to study digital
financial inclusion in rural Indonesia.

[Read more →](news.qmd)
:::
:::

:::

[All News & Events](news.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
